source_paper_title: Asymptotic Nash Equilibria of Finite-State Ergodic Markovian Mean Field Games
context:
Section 4.1, Remark 4.6 (page 14).

This paper does not address the convergence of exact Nash equilibria to mean field equilibria. Our method relies on the Lipschitz continuity of \(U_0\), the solution to the master equation. While the structure of the Nash system resembles that of the master equation, connecting the two falls outside the scope of this work. The first author is actively working on this problem, which remains open (see [8]).
title: Convergence of exact Markovian Nash equilibria to the mean field equilibrium in finite-state ergodic mean field games
background_latex:
- Let \,\([d]=\{1,\dots,d\}\) be a finite state space.
- Fix constants \,\(0<a_l<a_u<\infty\) and let \,\(A=[a_l,a_u]\subset\mathbb R_+\) be the admissible scalar rate range.
- For each player \,\(i\in[n]=\{1,\dots,n\}\), a Markovian (closed-loop) strategy is a measurable map \,\(\alpha^i:\mathbb R_+\times[d]^n\to\mathbb R^d\) such that if the current configuration is \,\(x=(x_1,\dots,x_n)\in[d]^n\), then for each \,\(y\neq x_i\) the jump rate from \,\(x_i\) to \,\(y\) equals \,\(\alpha^i_y(t,x)\in[a_l,a_u]\), and \,\(\alpha^i_{x_i}(t,x)=-\sum_{y\neq x_i}\alpha^i_y(t,x)\).
- Given a profile \,\(\alpha=(\alpha^1,\dots,\alpha^n)\), let \,\((X_t^{\alpha,1},\dots,X_t^{\alpha,n})\) be the induced continuous-time Markov chain on \,\([d]^n\).
- For player \,\(i\), define the empirical measure of the other players at time \,\(t\) by
\[\mu_t^{\alpha,i}:=\frac{1}{n-1}\sum_{j\in[n],\,j\neq i}\delta_{X_t^{\alpha,j}}\in\mathcal P([d]).\]
- Let the running cost be given by measurable functions \,\(f:[d]\times A_{[d]}^d\to\mathbb R\) and \,\(F:[d]\times\mathcal P([d])\to\mathbb R\), where \,\(A_{[d]}^d\) denotes the set of allowable rate vectors (as in the paper).
- The infinite-horizon ergodic cost to player \,\(i\) under profile \,\(\alpha\) is
\[J_0^{n,i}(\alpha):=\limsup_{T\to\infty}\frac{1}{T}\,\mathbb E\Big[\int_0^T \big(f(X_t^{\alpha,i},\alpha^i(t,X_t^{\alpha})) + F(X_t^{\alpha,i},\mu_t^{\alpha,i})\big)\,dt\Big].\]
- A profile \,\(\alpha\) is an \(\varepsilon\)-Nash equilibrium if for every \,\(i\) and every alternative Markovian strategy \,\(\beta^i\), one has \,\(J_0^{n,i}(\alpha)\le J_0^{n,i}([\alpha^{-i};\beta^i]) + \varepsilon\).
- The corresponding mean field game (MFG) admits a mean field equilibrium (MFE) described by a flow of measures \,\(\mu(t)\in\mathcal P([d])\) and an associated value \,\(\varrho\in\mathbb R\) (e.g., via the MFG system/master equation in the paper).
latex:
- Let \,\([d]=\{1,\dots,d\}\) be a finite state space.
- Fix constants \,\(0<a_l<a_u<\infty\) and let \,\(A=[a_l,a_u]\subset\mathbb R_+\) be the admissible scalar rate range.
- For each player \,\(i\in[n]=\{1,\dots,n\}\), a Markovian (closed-loop) strategy is a measurable map \,\(\alpha^i:\mathbb R_+\times[d]^n\to\mathbb R^d\) such that if the current configuration is \,\(x=(x_1,\dots,x_n)\in[d]^n\), then for each \,\(y\neq x_i\) the jump rate from \,\(x_i\) to \,\(y\) equals \,\(\alpha^i_y(t,x)\in[a_l,a_u]\), and \,\(\alpha^i_{x_i}(t,x)=-\sum_{y\neq x_i}\alpha^i_y(t,x)\).
- Given a profile \,\(\alpha=(\alpha^1,\dots,\alpha^n)\), let \,\((X_t^{\alpha,1},\dots,X_t^{\alpha,n})\) be the induced continuous-time Markov chain on \,\([d]^n\).
- For player \,\(i\), define the empirical measure of the other players at time \,\(t\) by
\[\mu_t^{\alpha,i}:=\frac{1}{n-1}\sum_{j\in[n],\,j\neq i}\delta_{X_t^{\alpha,j}}\in\mathcal P([d]).\]
- Let the running cost be given by measurable functions \,\(f:[d]\times A_{[d]}^d\to\mathbb R\) and \,\(F:[d]\times\mathcal P([d])\to\mathbb R\), where \,\(A_{[d]}^d\) denotes the set of allowable rate vectors (as in the paper).
- The infinite-horizon ergodic cost to player \,\(i\) under profile \,\(\alpha\) is
\[J_0^{n,i}(\alpha):=\limsup_{T\to\infty}\frac{1}{T}\,\mathbb E\Big[\int_0^T \big(f(X_t^{\alpha,i},\alpha^i(t,X_t^{\alpha})) + F(X_t^{\alpha,i},\mu_t^{\alpha,i})\big)\,dt\Big].\]
- A profile \,\(\alpha\) is an \(\varepsilon\)-Nash equilibrium if for every \,\(i\) and every alternative Markovian strategy \,\(\beta^i\), one has \,\(J_0^{n,i}(\alpha)\le J_0^{n,i}([\alpha^{-i};\beta^i]) + \varepsilon\).
- The corresponding mean field game (MFG) admits a mean field equilibrium (MFE) described by a flow of measures \,\(\mu(t)\in\mathcal P([d])\) and an associated value \,\(\varrho\in\mathbb R\) (e.g., via the MFG system/master equation in the paper).

Problem:
Problem: Establish a rigorous convergence theory, as \(n\to\infty\), for exact Markovian Nash equilibria \(\alpha^{(n)}\) of the \(n\)-player ergodic game to the mean field equilibrium \((\mu(\cdot),\varrho)\). Concretely, give verifiable assumptions under which:
- the empirical measure process \(\mu_t^{\alpha^{(n)}}:=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{j=1}^n\delta_{X_t^{\alpha^{(n)},j}}\) converges (in an appropriate sense, e.g. uniformly on compacts in probability) to \(\mu(t)\) solving the MFG Kolmogorov equation;
- the equilibrium values \(J_0^{n,i}(\alpha^{(n)})\) converge to \(\varrho\) (possibly with a rate);
- and/or the equilibrium feedbacks \(\alpha^{(n),i}(t,\cdot)\) converge to the MFE feedback (e.g. the master-equation selector \(\gamma^*(\cdot,\Delta U_0(\cdot,\mu(t)))\)).

Our method relies on the Lipschitz continuity of \(U_0\), the solution to the master equation. While the structure of the Nash system resembles that of the master equation, connecting the two falls outside the scope of this work. The first author is actively working on this problem, which remains open (see [8]).
